[`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a8e890106e82852c58aaa51ceff095275 "Returns the four vertices in counterclockwise order."), [`verticesContain`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#a0d982fcbd82c865bddc0ab1277aff7eb "Returns whether a point is one of the rectangle vertices."), [`width`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Rectangle.html#aed51c8fbbb4b4886ef1b907f6119b5b9 "Returns the rectangle width.").


### Disk

The class template [`Disk`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html "Closed Euclidean disk stored by boundary points plus optional disk label.") represents a circle with its interior. Disks are stored internally as three boundary points, in the same way as a [`Triangle`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Triangle.html "Closed triangle stored by three vertices."). This choice may be surprising, as the standard representation for disks is a center point and a radius. The main motivation is that the circumcircle of a triangle may be represented exactly for integers. Nevertheless, the constructor accepts both forms:

```C++
pgl::Disk d1({1,1}, {2,5}, {4,3}); // Disk from 3 points
pgl::Disk d2({2,3}, 4);            // Disk from a point and a radius
std::cout << d2 << std::endl;
// Output: Disk((-2,3)(6,3)(2,7))  // Output always uses 3 points
```

Disk does not have the `intersection` method and cannot be scaled on a single axis. A disk `d` has methods such as:

- [`d.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a19e32c8b7656bfe02982c8a40ea0ff57 "Returns whether the three boundary points are collinear."): Returns true if the points are collinear or equal.
- [`d.radius()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a69765084e501902a81583032fdb7c816 "Returns the center in this disk's exact coordinate type."): Returns the radius length.
- [`d.squaredRadius()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a0027c4ab0d85b7ff3e5b0d5b42b1745f "Returns the squared radius in an explicitly chosen result type."): Returns the squared radius.
- [`d.center()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a01f21dd5b971164474843df4ad71bfc1 "Returns the center (circumcenter of the three boundary points) in an explicitly chosen coordinate type."): Returns the center point.
- [`d.diameter()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ad7c86ba7778cf349bcb6c653e0470ba5 "Returns the horizontal diameter as a segment through the center."): As always returns a diameter [`Segment`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Segment.html "Unoriented closed segment between two endpoints plus optional segment label."), but for disks the segment is always horizontal.

- Other methods: [`a`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aab80a3c4810748f39fdc8c7e020eb232 "Returns the first boundary point (lexicographically smallest)."), [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aa4f2837f14655fd73098513f769e41da "Returns the squared radius in this disk's exact result type."), [`b`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aeb247fff089ba4d4463418f84f0f9e3d "Returns the second boundary point in canonical order."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a82543add1cf92f0b0116fd798ddecced "Returns the exact axis-aligned bounding box in the coordinate type."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aaec2b4934b1b89530f0ea126a080f4ef "Returns an iterator to the first boundary point."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a190ee43500b52f235991dc9bc3095180 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`c`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a20d60679286b84a64063b648c860430e "Returns the third boundary point in canonical order."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ad39c1b05f308f292112590c388575a36 "Returns a const iterator to the first boundary point."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a5c10cddfceeff5a571f4b892ba2e11ad "Returns a const iterator past the last boundary point."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a40627a86d5eb68250e7daba7a1ca36db "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a3afd10decc72c4a11747024bee9c635b "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a4245360f58a74d7969b4176627797e8f "Returns an iterator past the last boundary point."), [`fbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#aff00091cb8c1a2afd46f55e31fb4d38f "Returns a floating-point bounding box of the disk."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#afd129f4cb8111846762e2b4ca06db7c1 "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ab6e63f207efba3178e1a665c70f60cd0 "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if no boundary point equals point."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a5c43e8fa09ae818be5fa70d2f18c9474 "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a7932e3c1de11bf765ca378582df887e6 "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a04a545293c1496c34b4bf601a124eff6 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`label`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#af5f96f809e41fead49b9cafa8de11938 "Returns the disk label."), [`pointInside`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a78477a3bcf69c6f36cff63e5a65442e3 "Returns the midpoint of the first two boundary points."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a8d38ec21e40e6459475c0006646d352b "Rotates the disk by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a07288ad0eb805ea8a8e6a67d5b8e497c "Returns the disk rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#ae384cc53e8754c9618a33a6a0d58945c "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Disk.html#a9276993379a1097fb9a2c3464faf66ed "Returns the number of stored boundary points (always 3).").


### Polygon

The class template [`Polygon`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html "Closed simple polygon stored by its vertices.") represents a simple polygon. It can be constructed for any number of points in a container that must be given in the order they appear on the polygon. The vertices are accessed in counterclockwise order starting from the minimum vertex (minimum x, breaking ties by minimum y). ~~Internally, the polygon is stored as multiple x-monotone polylines for improved performance.~~

A polygon `P` has methods such as:

- [`P.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af1d297b10aee661458543c2f5fa00645 "Checks if the polygon is degenerate (has zero area)."): Returns true if the polygon has null area.
- [`P.isSimple()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a5e182cf106f6be5ed3fb49c07daf80c6 "Tests whether the polygon is simple (its boundary does not touch or cross itself)."): Returns true if the edges only intersect at the endpoints of consecutive edges. Takes $O(n \log n)$ time for $n$ edges.

- Other methods: [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ad4765e3f6390f06a59f25fef4fcb219f "Computes the area of the polygon."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a1ff93373098baf44e68ba00bb02565d6 "Computes the bounding box of the polygon."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a9411cd18c37f8f81740ce5ad1cf834aa "Returns a constant iterator to the first vertex."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a27e7ae09110e8b6d3a0c3b18885d9302 "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a15bd64aed079105234fc3148afb5e1be "Returns a constant iterator to the first vertex."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af6668ee498e370013d29d775d0b1973b "Returns a constant iterator past the last vertex."), [`centroid`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a66951f26f6ab36fa08fc6bd0188d3234 "Computes the area-weighted centroid of the polygon."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a3a46d062f49f67213b208549e06dd23c "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a7fb8c1a9d66a86a6feaf5da1de83ea9b "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`diameter`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a34513cb8bcf668d052f7d15ad58cfe85 "Returns a segment realizing the diameter (the farthest vertex pair)."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ad8456b5d296bc23059b0ffe7053dd74d "Returns the edges of the polygon."), [`edgesBegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#aa874eea1865d33c7d88d2820cf959cd3 "Returns an iterator to the first unoriented edge."), [`edgesEnd`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a8e2ead5cd51c5412f672f17cf1cbeb68 "Returns an iterator past the last unoriented edge."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af74dcdb08aba7a122770a1de6f9aa1d9 "Returns a constant iterator past the last vertex."), [`fbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a6088100ec92a2d72b8732e2e12afa1b8 "Computes the floating-point bounding box of the polygon."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae81b4e6f9129d731d6732f8b20bd45db "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end. Useful for iterating polygon edges where the last edge wraps around."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a4242053f262a712d1e38cc5ced8e301b "Returns the smallest index i with (*this)[i] == point, or -1 if point is not a vertex."), [`interiorContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a28a4948401349722af11f2d72259c08f "Tests whether this shape's interior contains the other shape (A∖∂A ⊇ B)."), [`interiorsIntersect`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#aef20609eba315abe5d525bc5ef565c0b "Tests whether the interiors of the two shapes intersect ((A∖∂A) ∩ (B∖∂B) ≠ ∅)."), [`intersection`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ac313537771c4ba4055d0295cbbb70df5 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint."), [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae0a54047480c7f2623728dae37fbddc3 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅)."), [`label`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ad5fee5791a7d7f6e48a6bd1dea3f446b "Returns the polygon label."), [`orientedEdges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ab6c6a1c32666c9f220ba03c2c4864f98 "Returns the oriented edges of the polygon."), [`orientedEdgesBegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#aa6a00964980269056093f52df5ca2302 "Returns an iterator to the first oriented edge."), [`orientedEdgesEnd`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ab78f1bd5cba565f6024ce5d08e41db8c "Returns an iterator past the last oriented edge."), [`rotate90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#abae5b3272afe3c706ff1378fb6d00683 "Rotates the polygon by 90k degrees around the origin in place."), [`rotated90`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae737bfc7fb10dc9fbe6f9101dad4edc6 "Returns the polygon rotated by 90k degrees around the origin."), [`scaleDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#afcc813c8d7e00a28290e5e535de205d7 "Divides the polygon's x-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a8161a2a6e3c75c8eb5f3b6ca0659e9f4 "Divides the polygon's y-coordinates by a divisor in place."), [`scaleUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a7485d53c8396b8402102695e9f122255 "Multiplies the polygon's x-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaleUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#abdd5508e61d4c797f02257116e2d3125 "Multiplies the polygon's y-coordinates by a factor in place."), [`scaledDownX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#aeeff4e7fc91d48475d65b9d7bb263733 "Returns the polygon with its x-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledDownY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a74f9e5944239271b44cf12a4fdc6788a "Returns the polygon with its y-coordinates divided by a divisor."), [`scaledUpX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae6f150525d7140e673bcd0089816d38f "Returns the polygon with its x-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`scaledUpY`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a3578b5ad99b36b49160c36bdfdf82b8d "Returns the polygon with its y-coordinates multiplied by a factor."), [`separates`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af5ef3e311c9b72c159e20de09fe8c8ef "Tests whether removing this shape disconnects the other shape (B∖A is disconnected)."), [`size`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#a9a70b70146923bd677bebf7ee9ef38b8 "Returns the number of vertices in the polygon."), [`twiceArea`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ae08d1b6ee40568069ed6ace905abca0f "Computes twice the (unsigned) area of the polygon via the shoelace formula."), [`vertices`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#ac441c37b1bb195da9f14784e2f6207bf "Returns the vertices of the polygon (translation applied)."), [`verticesCentroid`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Polygon.html#af868c2942503c2bafaf89f64af1fe463 "Computes the centroid of the vertex set (the average of the vertices).").


### Convex

The class template [`Convex`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html "Closed convex polygon stored by its vertices.") represents a convex polygon. It can be constructed for any number of points in a container and will construct the convex hull. The vertices are stored in counterclockwise order starting from the minimum vertex (minimum x, breaking ties by minimum y). If the container already has the vertices in order, a second constructor parameter can be set to true to avoid computing the convex hull.

A convex polygon `c` has methods such as:

- [`c.isDegenerate()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a460c2cbe73f75e60d0153be2acb1662d "Checks if the convex polygon is degenerate (has zero area)."): Returns true if the convex polygon has null area.
- [`c.centroid()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad4000c6649317b13e2695a209d9102e8 "Computes the centroid of the convex polygon."): Returns the centroid.
- `c.insert(s)`: Enlarges the convex polygon in order to contain a finite shape `s`. The shape must expose its vertices.
- `c.insert(points)`: Enlarges the convex polygon in order to contain every point in the input range.
- `c.upperHull()`: Returns the upper monotone polyline.
- `c.lowerHull()`: Returns the lower monotone polyline.

It knows how to convert itself with an explicit cast to:
- `(pgl::Polygon) c` or [`c.asPolygon()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ab08ccc569a03b8056db708b1f4e26e2f "Returns the convex polygon as a simple polygon."): Returns the polygon representation of the convex polygon.

If the convex polygon `c` has $n$ vertices, then:

- [`c.diameter()`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad44a3c0e434930328c723fcae6c4c7c9 "Returns a segment realizing the diameter (the farthest vertex pair).") takes $O(n)$ time.
- [`c.intersects(s)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\log n)$ time if `s` is a shape with $O(1)$ vertices (not including Disk).
- [`s.intersects(c)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\log n)$ time if `s` is a shape with $O(1)$ vertices (not including Disk).
- [`c.intersects(c2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).") takes $O(\min(n+m) \log(n+m))$ time if `c2` is a convex polygon with $m$ vertices.
- Other predicates take the same time as [`intersects`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a0717c549a9515d6c65cf68e620361239 "Tests whether this shape and the other shape intersect (A ∩ B ≠ ∅).").
- [`c.intersection(c2)`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ab48484ad2bc85d3c818ce3e62c2d5af6 "Returns the intersection of the two shapes (A ∩ B), empty when they are disjoint.") takes $O((n+m) log (n+m))$ time if `c2` is a convex polygon with $m$ vertices.

- Other methods: [`antipodalPairs`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ad8daeec7e468c1b3a12be81c96752f95 "Returns every antipodal vertex-index pair, via rotating calipers."), [`area`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a75a11db621ea33f8ea69f4946de7034e "Computes the area of the convex polygon."), [`bbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a06e8f55ebcb8cf814bbc5df5d4288312 "Computes the bounding box of the convex polygon."), [`begin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ac3d6626a1da2b4dcb4723679ba2d36b4 "Returns a constant iterator to the beginning of vertices."), [`boundaryContains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#afd902656ee3c6a53a741ba7325248f0e "Tests whether this shape's boundary contains the other shape (∂A ⊇ B)."), [`cbegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#af6878c6b999ac61e7e2328c71fc1a86a "Returns a constant iterator to the beginning of vertices."), [`cend`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a196e2ec7cfe538a2a4e39f8ed93b9a96 "Returns a constant iterator to the end of vertices."), [`contains`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#abbd6d5e37f4cb4ac7c3af412fe851ad3 "Tests whether this shape contains the other shape (A ⊇ B)."), [`crosses`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a650d1e04ed66ea74364cd7ca974879ff "Tests whether the two shapes mutually separate each other (each disconnects the other)."), [`edges`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a9dcf1c9ee107a02ec9893e2baa59620c "Returns the edges of the convex polygon."), [`edgesAtX`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ac8afcc0efcc66b471d0c3a5f994d204b "Returns two edges of the convex polygon that intersect with the vertical line at x."), [`edgesBegin`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a319a670fa0703ffd3f263268a7fddfd6 "Returns an iterator to the first unoriented edge."), [`edgesEnd`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a47390af7cb9472bcde6c3c0e10290f37 "Returns an iterator past the last unoriented edge."), [`end`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#ab4c6a1493d20d239550d07fb87cb260f "Returns a constant iterator to the end of vertices."), [`fbox`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a129c434124d3e5fa172ae7b12993456b "Computes the floating-point bounding box of the convex polygon."), [`get`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a20a4c5a95a53f91958478a8f40908e0e "Cyclic access: same as operator[] but index is taken modulo size(); negative indices wrap from the end. Useful for iterating polygon edges where the last edge wraps around."), [`index`](https://gfonsecabr.github.io/pgl/structpgl_1_1Convex.html#a66ca8f0c5c1fb858dc88134abe664207 "Returns the smallest
